what_it_does: This file installs a runtime icon renderer on the global DNA object. It reads an icon pack from DNA.iconPack.provides.icons, resolves names or substrate-type aliases to custom icon definitions, and exposes two render paths: DNA.iconSVG returns an SVG markup string, and DNA.renderIcon returns a mountable span-wrapped SVG element. It also exposes DNA.iconName for alias resolution and DNA.hasIcon for presence checks.
observations: Wraps everything in a strict-mode IIFE that attaches to window.DNA.
Expects DNA.iconPack.provides.icons and supplies a fallback pack if missing.
DNA.iconName resolves aliases first, then falls back to identity.
defOf tries the aliased key and then the original name in p.custom.
DNA.iconSVG builds an SVG string with configurable size, color, stroke width, and default viewBox 0 0 24 24.
DNA.renderIcon creates a span with inline styles and injects the SVG string via innerHTML.
Class names used are 'dna-ic' for the svg and 'dna-ic-wrap' for the span.
standards: Dual-output API: string builder face (iconSVG) and DOM element face (renderIcon).
Explicitly designed to mirror composition's icon runtime shape.
conventions: Defensive fallback object when expected pack is absent.
Default parameter handling via o || {} and || fallbacks.
Inline style assignment for the wrapper span.
concerns: innerHTML is used to inject d.svg without sanitization; a malicious or malformed icon pack could introduce unsafe markup.
No validation that d.svg is actually SVG content.
Dependency on a specific global DNA.iconPack shape; if the shape differs the renderer silently degrades to empty output.
renderIcon applies inline display styles that may be hard to override without !important.
notes: A small bridge renderer that lets the same DNA icon pack feed both string-generating and direct-mount consumers.
questions: Where and when is DNA.iconPack populated?
What is the exact schema for aliases, defaults, and custom icon definitions?
Is d.svg trusted/validated upstream, given the innerHTML usage?
What does 'shell-and-sockets single-colour model' imply for multi-colour icons?
How does composition's runtime physically consume this same pack?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it reads a DNA icon pack, resolves names/aliases, and provides both SVG-string and DOM-element renderers themed by currentColor.
apparent_themes: runtime bridge between DNA and composition icon systems
icon-as-data driven by a shared pack
dual consumption patterns from one source
intention_signals: The file is trying to make DNA and a separate composition icon runtime interchangeable by consuming the exact same icon pack, so no conversion layer is needed when composition code is handed over.
novelty: Distinctive in providing both a string-builder face and a mountable DOM face from the same pack definition, plus substrate-type alias resolution.
contribution: Provides a runtime icon renderer that consumes a DNA icon pack and emits both SVG markup strings and mountable DOM elements, supporting two downstream consumption styles without altering the pack format.
summary_for_embedding: A DNA-namespaced runtime icon renderer that reads a shared icon pack and produces either an SVG string or a span-wrapped SVG element by icon name or substrate alias.
intention_for_embedding: To let DNA and composition icon runtimes share one icon pack with no glue, serving both string-builder and direct-DOM consumers.

symbols.does: Returns the active icon pack definitions from DNA.iconPack, or a fallback empty pack.
symbols.description: A local helper that normalizes access to DNA.iconPack.provides.icons. It returns the pack's library, defaults, aliases, and custom icons; if the pack is absent it supplies a safe fallback so downstream functions can still operate without throwing.

symbols.does: Resolves a name or substrate-type alias to a custom icon definition object.
symbols.description: Uses DNA.iconName to follow alias cascades, then looks up the resolved key in the pack's custom icons map, falling back to the original name if needed. Returns the icon definition or null when no match exists.
